libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Write Scala tests that exercise org.apache.pekko.macros.LogHelper / LogHelperMacro. Because LogHelper is private[pekko], test code that extends or directly uses it should live in package org.apache.pekko. Avoid the old org.apache.pekko.http.ccompat pre213/since213 classes; they are not present in pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1.

deterministic_setup.reason: pekko-parsing_2.13 declares pekko-actor_2.13 as a provided dependency, and LogHelper references org.apache.pekko.event.LoggingAdapter and Pekko annotations.
pekko-parsing_2.13 declares scala-reflect as a provided dependency, and LogHelperMacro uses scala.reflect.macros.blackbox.Context.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8878 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The artifact exposes mostly internal/private Pekko macro support, so public documentation is sparse and tests may need package-level access rather than normal external-user APIs.
Existing 1.3.0 tests use ccompat macro annotations that were removed from the 2.0.0-M1 jar, so carrying those tests forward will fail at compile time.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The resolved 2.0.0-M1 artifact is a small Scala macro/helper jar whose meaningful APIs reference provided dependencies that are not pulled transitively by a normal test scaffold.
